description_long: This object is a stylized pink flower with six broad, rounded petals arranged in a radial pattern around a central cluster of stamens. The petals are a vibrant pink, fading slightly toward the center, and are smooth and slightly curved. At the center, there are several yellow and red spherical structures representing the stamens and pistils, adding a touch of realism and color contrast. The flower is attached to a short, cylindrical green stem, which is simple and untextured. No leaves are visible on or near the stem, giving the flower a clean, minimalistic appearance. The overall design is simple yet visually appealing, making it suitable for decorative or illustrative purposes in urban environments such as parks or gardens.
description: A stylized pink flower with six rounded petals, a central cluster of yellow and red stamens, and a short green stem. The design is clean and minimalistic, with no visible leaves.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full radial arrangement of six pink petals and the central cluster of yellow and red stamens, with the green stem visible below.
description_view_1: Angled side view highlighting the thickness and curvature of the petals, as well as the protruding central stamens and the green stem extending backward.
description_view_2: Side profile view displaying the flatness of the petals and the vertical arrangement of the central stamens, with the stem barely visible.
description_view_3: Angled rear view showing the back sides of the petals and the central stamens, with the green stem extending outward.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The petals are arranged in a radial, whorled configuration, with each petal evenly spaced around the central axis. There is clear radial symmetry, and the petals slightly overlap at their bases.
